summary: Find a magnetic-field-aligned NV from image172647, then obtain a well-supported T2star and 13C conclusion.

human_request_preview: Objective: Find a magnetic-field-aligned NV from image172647, then obtain a well-supported T2star and 13C conclusion. Start a project using the spatial range of <MATLAB_23C_ROOT>\SavedImages\3DXYZ-Image-2026-05-14-172647.mat as the initial search region. Identify an NV aligned to the magnetic field within that image range; if none is found, search different regions. Judge magnetic-field alignment using a strong-pi pulsed ODMR scan; if it does not show a clear usable resonance, reject

summary: Superseded and moved stale queued duplicate pODMR job for old image172647_c01 to failed before execution. The job came from interrupted old single-submit batch image172647_c01 after that candidate had already failed low-count/retrack and project state moved to reimage1804_c01. No hardware was touched by the superseded queued job.

Current reimage1804_c01 strong-pi pODMR remains running. Status at elapsed 592 s: phase run_experiment_scan_point, average_index=1/4, final_counts_text=39.551 kcps, autosave enabled but live average results not yet available. Queue is empty after stale c01 cleanup.

Raw-exported first autosaved average of the running reimage1804_c01 strong-pi pODMR. XML review confirms readout 1 is mS=0 reference and readout 2 is signal with full_expt=0. First-average snapshot shows only provisional few-percent fluctuations versus the 22% expected strong-pi contrast; not a terminal alignment/rejection verdict. Wait for full 4-average terminal result.

Bridge-free conditional Ramsey/T2star/13C follow-up plan prepared while pODMR runs. It calculates B and expected 13C Larmor from the ms=+1 resonance, checks an 8 us / 51 point Ramsey FFT grid with det=2 MHz, and records prerequisite gates before any execute.
Note summarizing the conditional Ramsey/T2star/13C model plan and branch logic after terminal pODMR review.

next_action.agent_prompt: Treat this as an event-driven research wake. Read NV_RESEARCH_MEMORY.md startup memory first, then brief.md, human_advice.md, work/state.md, and compact project state. Use the Memory Index to read relevant sections from NV_RESEARCH_KNOWLEDGE.md only as needed. Read .manager/evidence.jsonl, project policy, detailed backlog entries, and directly relevant evidence only as needed, then use the same-wake runnable work loop to complete, update, or queue all currently runnable safe project work until a real stop condition is reached. Python should stay limited to state, queue safety, audit logs, and hard boundaries; the agent should own the scientific plan. Process advice inbox messages with list-advice-inbox/dispose-advice when present, record evidence, update backlog, write work/state.md, log.md, and work/notes files as useful, update NV_RESEARCH_KNOWLEDGE.md for reusable detailed lessons, and write the completion marker.
